paper_id: do Nascimento and de Oliveira - 2023 - An Ontology for Context Modeling in Smart Spaces

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology was implemented in OWL language using Prot´ eg´ e software [17], version 5.5.0. The implementation used the gUFO [4] as base ontology.
The SpaceCOn ontology has been used in an architecture for context integration described in Fig. 8.

Agents communicate using messages. The messages use terms defined by the SpaceCOn ontology. For example, query requests contain a set of instances of the ContextualElement class, and responses to applications contain a set of instances of contextual elements modeled through the ContextualElementInstance class.
This ontology can be a strategic tool to integrate context data from different sources in large smart environments, such as smart universities and cities.

goals.justification: The ontology was actually implemented in OWL/Protégé and deployed in a context integration architecture where agents communicate using its terms. It serves as an instrument enabling interoperability and integration of context data across systems.

evidence_quotes.quote: Entities, aspects, contextual elements, categories, and QoC parameters represent terms whose semantics need to be clearly defined so that agents in a smart space can exchange information based on a common vocabulary.
evidence_quotes.section: 3 Formal Context Definition Used in This Work

evidence_quotes.quote: An ontology defines a common vocabulary within a specific domain. It includes a set of semantic definitions that are interpretable by computer programs where the concepts of a domain and the relationships among them are defined [19].
This paper aims to present an ontology called SpaceCOn that allows semantically representing context information in smart environments.

goals.justification: The ontology is explicitly framed as defining a common vocabulary and shared semantics for context concepts in smart spaces, supporting shared understanding of the domain among agents/systems.

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology must also support the representation of historical contexts.

uncertain_goals.reason: Historical context representation is a feature of the ontology's application data, not a stated purpose of the model itself as documentation/reference.
